etymology.description: 謝(謝) (xiè, "thank") repeated to emphasize gratitude, 你(你) (nǐ, "you") specifying the person being thanked.
etymology.interpretation: In 谢谢你, the character 謝(謝) (xiè, "thank") is a gesture of gratitude. By repeating 謝(謝) (xiè, "thank"), it intensifies the expression of thanks. 你(你) (nǐ, "you") identifies the recipient of this gratitude, creating a direct and personalized expression of appreciation.

examples.chinese: 谢谢你给我打电话(謝謝你給我打電話)。
examples.pinyin: Xièxie nǐ gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà.

examples.chinese: 今天谢谢你帮我(今天謝謝你幫我)，我真的很感激(我真的很感激)。
examples.pinyin: Jīntiān xièxie nǐ bāng wǒ, wǒ zhēn de hěn gǎnjī.
examples.english: Thank you for helping me today, I am really grateful.

examples.chinese: 谢谢你(謝謝你)，这个礼物很棒(這個禮物很棒)。
examples.pinyin: Xièxie nǐ, zhè ge lǐwù hěn bàng.
examples.english: Thank you, this gift is great.
